steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states volunteering helps society overall. But the survey contradicts this perception: most young people recognize only individual benefits, while only 6% see societal value. This gap between what is true and what most young people believe is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice B works because the survey reveals young people underestimate volunteering's impact—only 6% see the societal benefit that the passage claims is real. This gap means volunteering is more helpful than they think. The others miss the mark: A treats individual/societal as geography (not the issue), C invents a magnitude ranking not in the data, and D shifts from perception to access barriers. That matches choice B — B is correct.
